thought.text:
Note: scoring just compares stdout and return code. We don't need to match clang's IR text exactly. Now let me design the EmitIR. We need to handle:

- Types: int (i32), void, char, long, long long, pointer, array, function
- Expressions: IntegerLiteral, StringLiteral, DeclRefExpr, ParenExpr, UnaryExpr (+/-/!), BinaryExpr (all 16 ops including short-circuit), CallExpr, InitListExpr, ImplicitInitExpr, ImplicitCastExpr (LValueToRValue, IntegralCast, ArrayToPointerDecay, FunctionToPointerDecay, NoOp)
- Statements: NullStmt, DeclStmt, ExprStmt, CompoundStmt, IfStmt, WhileStmt, DoStmt, BreakStmt, ContinueStmt, ReturnStmt
- Declarations: VarDecl (local & global, scalar & array & with init), FunctionDecl

Let me look at the test cases categories first to understand the scope:

action.command: cd /workspace/YatCC && cmake --build build -t task3-score 2>&1 | grep -E "总分|/100\.00 \[" | tail -30

tool_call.name: terminal
tool_call.arguments: {"command": "cd /workspace/YatCC && cmake --build build -t task3-score 2>&1 | grep -E \"\u603b\u5206|/100\\.00 \\[\" | tail -30", "security_risk": "MEDIUM", "summary": "Run task3 baseline to gauge initial state"}

summary: Run task3 baseline to gauge initial state
